invariant: BF1 — never overwrite a frozen artifact; a change is a new version + change request
owning_role: SYNTHESIZE (00-aprd/SYNTHESIZE.md) — versions to a NEW aprd.v<N+1>.frozen.md + re-signs the lock; leaves the baseline aprd.frozen.md byte-identical. 'Touching aprd.frozen.md is the one fatal error this stage exists to avoid.'
seed: overlay defects/frozen-overwrite/aprd.frozen.mutated.md → .aprd/aprd.frozen.md (baseline mutated in place: tag jammed into v1 instead of versioning)
load_bearing_assertion: A correctly-run feature-add NEVER writes the baseline aprd.frozen.md; new content goes to aprd.v2.frozen.md. The mutated-v1 overlay is the illegal state.

e2e_validated.prevention_proven: clean-room SYNTHESIZE golden run left .aprd/aprd.frozen.md BYTE-IDENTICAL (md5 unchanged) while producing aprd.v2.frozen.md + re-signing the lock v1→v2 (status frozen). BF1 prevention discipline holds.
e2e_validated.freeze_gate_proven: clean-room SYNTHESIZE run on a bench with aprd.lock status='draft' tripped escape #3 ('feature-add but baseline aprd.frozen.md/aprd.lock not present+frozen') → HALT, wrote nothing. Cannot extend an unfrozen baseline.

detection_caveat: Mechanical detection of a POST-HOC mutated-yet-still-frozen v1 (the overlay) is NOT exercised by a sha-recompute: the locks carry NOMINAL content_sha256 values (oracle/lock convention: 'consumers check status+manifest, do NOT recompute'; both runners noted the v1 lock sha f3a1c0... ≠ the file's actual hash). BF1 is therefore enforced by (a) the rule — no stage writes a frozen artifact, (b) SYNTHESIZE's version-to-new-file discipline, (c) the freeze-gate. A byte-equality/integrity verifier with a REAL recorded sha (or git-tracked baseline diff) is what would mechanically reject the mutated-v1 overlay.
